steps.title: State Bakhtin's main claim
steps.content: The text defines fabula and syuzhet, illustrates them with The Godfather Part II, and then turns to Bakhtin's objection. His central claim is that those two concepts are insufficient to completely describe a narrative because they miss subtler interactions among artist, work, and audience.

steps.title: Reject narrower claims
steps.content: B overstates the point into a claim about different audience interpretations. C makes the film example the main idea and says the concepts clarify it, while the text uses the example before criticizing the concepts' limits. D makes an unsupported claim about chronological order. The answer is A.
